request.question: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

hypothesis.readiness_rationale: Threshold and mechanistic rationale are missing, but the intervention, biological system, comparator, and measurable outcome are clear.
clarifying_questions: What is the numeric success threshold for improvement in post-thaw viability?

hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Does adding 250mM trehalose to freezing medium improve HeLa post-thaw viability vs DMSO?

references.relevance_note: This study investigates the use of trehalose in a cryopreservation context, although it focuses on hydrogel microspheres rather than HeLa cells.

literature_qc.explanation: In the context of intervention, trehalose is mentioned in paper [4], where it is utilized in the cryopreservation of cell-laden hydrogel microspheres. While the biological system differs, the exploration of trehalose as a cryoprotectant is relevant. The experiment type of comparative cryopreservation aligns with several studies, including paper [1], which discusses chemical approaches in cryopreservation, although not specifically about HeLa cells. Therefore, there is related work but not an exact match.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Related publications exist. Review the references, cite relevant prior work in your plan, and explicitly state how your experiment differs or extends the existing approach.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with 250 mM trehalose versus DMSO as cryoprotectants.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be cryopreserved using two different treatments: one with 250 mM trehalose and another with DMSO. Cells will be subjected to controlled cooling rates and then thawed. Post-thaw viability will be assessed using trypan blue exclusion method after a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C. Each treatment will be replicated in triplicate to ensure statistical reliability.
controls: DMSO as the standard cryoprotectant at a concentration of 10% (v/v).
HeLa cells without cryoprotectant, subjected to the same freezing and thawing process without any protective agents.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare the freezing medium by mixing 500 mL of DMEM containing 10% (v/v) FBS and 1% (v/v) penicillin-streptomycin at room temperature.
Expected outcome: Homogeneous freezing medium is prepared without visibility of particulates.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare the trehalose solution at a concentration of 250 mM by dissolving 68.5 g of trehalose dihydrate (Sigma Aldrich) in 1 L of ultrapure water at room temperature.
Expected outcome: 250 mM trehalose solution is fully dissolved and clear.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Reagent Preparation
Aliquot 1 mL of DMSO (10% v/v) in a sterile cryogenic tube.
Expected outcome: 1 mL aliquots of DMSO are prepared and stored on ice.
Safety: DMSO is a hazardous material; wear gloves.

DAY 1 | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in T75 flasks in DMEM with 10% FBS and 1% penicillin-streptomycin until they reach 80-90% confluence (approximately 48 h).
Expected outcome: HeLa cells reach 80-90% confluence in culture flasks with no contamination detected.

DAY 1 | 10 min | Cell Trypan Blue Staining
Trypsinize HeLa cells with 5 mL of trypsin-EDTA for 5 min at 37°C to detach cells from the flask surface.
Expected outcome: HeLa cells are successfully detached and resuspended in culture medium.

DAY 1 | 15 min | DMSO/Trehalose Treatment
Resuspend 1 × 10^6 cells in 1 mL of freezing medium containing either 250 mM trehalose or 10% DMSO and transfer to cryogenic vials.
Expected outcome: Cells are evenly distributed in the cryogenic vials with adequate volume of cryoprotectant.

DAY 1 | 2 h | Cryopreservation
Cool cells at a rate of -1°C/min from room temperature to -80°C using a Mr. Frosty freezing container.
Expected outcome: Cells reach -80°C without freezing damage, protected by trehalose or DMSO.

DAY 2 | 2 min | Thawing
Thaw the cryopreserved cells rapidly by immersing the cryogenic vials in a 37°C water bath until just melted (approx. 1-2 min).
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed without ice crystals remaining.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Post-Thaw Recovery
Incubate the thawed cells in 5 mL of fresh DMEM containing 10% FBS at 37°C for 30 min to recover.
Expected outcome: Cells recover for 30 min before viability assessment.

DAY 2 | 20 min | Trypan Blue Viability Assay
Add 100 µL of 0.4% trypan blue solution to 100 µL of recovered cell suspension and mix gently to perform the viability assay using a hemocytometer.
Expected outcome: Determine viable cell count per mL; number of viable cells should be calculated for statistical comparison.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Statistical Analysis
Perform statistical analysis using ANOVA to compare post-thaw viability between trehalose and DMSO treatment groups, using a significance level of p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined between treatments, with p-values reported.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: HeLa cells can tolerate the freezing-thawing process with both cryoprotectants.
Post-thaw viability can be accurately assessed using trypan blue exclusion.
The cooling rate of -1°C/min is suitable for both treatments.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability of trehalose group is significantly higher (p < 0.05) compared to DMSO group.
Generate viable cells counts > 70% for both treatments to ensure no major freezing damage occurred.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot number of trehalose and DMSO for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (≤10) throughout experiments.
Ensure that incubator CO2 levels and temperatures are calibrated before use.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. Add Mr. Frosty step
3. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: >=10 percentage points (pilot fallback) improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with 250 mM trehalose versus DMSO as cryoprotectants.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
